story.iWant: a registry that resolves agent role prompt templates with a multi-level fallback chain and variable interpolation
story.soThat: each agent role gets the correct system prompt preamble based on configuration, provider overrides, and built-in defaults -- and the engine can render dynamic content into those templates

task.title: Define built-in role prompt templates and constants

tasks.task: - [ ] Create `packages/providers/src/agent-prompt-registry.ts` - [ ] Define `BUILTIN_TEMPLATES` as `Object.freeze(Partial<Record<AgentType, string>>)` with templates for all 9 roles: architect, implementer, reviewer, tester, analyst, scrum_master, researcher, planner, documenter - [ ] Define `GENERIC_FALLBACK` constant for roles with no built-in template - [ ] Define `MAX_TEMPLATE_LENGTH = 1_000_000` (1MB) and `MAX_VAR_VALUE_LENGTH = 100_000` (100KB) size limit constants - [ ] Define `FORBIDDEN_KEYS` set with `__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype` - [ ] Define `IAgentPromptRegistry` interface with `render()`, `resolveTemplate()`, `registerBuiltin()` methods - [ ] Define `AgentPromptRegistryOptions` interface with `config`, optional `logger`, optional `immutableRoles` - [ ] Templates use `{{variable}}` placeholder syntax (not template literals) - [ ] Extract prompt preambles from `packages/orchestrator/src/engine.ts` generatePlan() and implementCode() - [ ] Write unit tests verifying built-in templates exist for all 9 expected roles

task.title: Implement template resolution chain (6 levels) with security guards

tasks.task: - [ ] Implement `AgentPromptRegistry` class implementing `IAgentPromptRegistry` with constructor accepting `AgentPromptRegistryOptions` - [ ] Copy `BUILTIN_TEMPLATES` into `Object.create(null)` prototype-free instance field `builtinTemplates` for mutability - [ ] Implement `resolveTemplate(role: AgentType, providerName: string): string` with 6-level chain: 1. `roles[role].providerPrompts?.[providerName]` (guarded with `Object.hasOwn()`) 2. `roles[role].systemPrompt` 3. `defaults.providerPrompts?.[providerName]` (guarded with `Object.hasOwn()`) 4. `defaults.systemPrompt` 5. `builtinTemplates[role]` 6. `GENERIC_FALLBACK` - [ ] Validate `providerName` against `FORBIDDEN_KEYS` in `resolveTemplate()` - [ ] Guard against undefined `roles`, missing `providerPrompts` map, and missing keys at every step - [ ] Implement `registerBuiltin(role: string, template: string): void` with: - Prototype pollution guard (reject `FORBIDDEN_KEYS`) - Immutable roles protection (reject roles in `immutableRoles` set) - Audit trail logging (WARN on override, INFO on new) - [ ] Write unit tests for each of the 6 resolution levels, null-safety, security guards, and edge cases

task.title: Implement render() with {{variable}} interpolation and size limits

tasks.task: - [ ] Implement `render(role: AgentType, providerName: string, vars: Record<string, string>): string` - [ ] `render()` calls `resolveTemplate()` then `interpolate()` - [ ] Implement private `interpolate(template: string, vars: Record<string, string>): string` - [ ] Use `split('{{key}}').join(value)` -- NOT regex -- for safety on untrusted template content - [ ] Skip variables whose values exceed `MAX_VAR_VALUE_LENGTH` (100KB) with warning log - [ ] Truncate rendered templates exceeding `MAX_TEMPLATE_LENGTH` (1MB) with warning log - [ ] Unreferenced `{{placeholders}}` are left unchanged (safe default -- not removed, not throwing) - [ ] Document iterative expansion behavior: variables applied in Object.entries() order, indirect expansion is possible - [ ] Write unit tests: basic interpolation, multiple vars, empty vars, unreplaced placeholders, special regex chars in values, size limits, empty-string template override - [ ] Create test file `packages/providers/src/agent-prompt-registry.test.ts`

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Class is named `AgentPromptRegistry` implementing `IAgentPromptRegistry` interface (NOT `PromptRegistry` -- that name is taken by `packages/mcp-client/src/registry.ts` for MCP prompt management)

`IAgentPromptRegistry` interface is exported with `render()`, `resolveTemplate()`, `registerBuiltin()` methods (pattern matches IProviderHealthTracker (9-3), IAgentProviderFactory (9-4), IProviderChain (9-5))
`resolveTemplate()` implements the full 6-level resolution chain: per-provider-per-role -> per-role -> per-provider-default -> global default -> builtin -> generic
`render()` replaces `{{variable}}` placeholders with provided values using split+join (not regex)
`render()` with empty vars leaves `{{placeholders}}` unchanged (safe default)
Unmatched `{{placeholders}}` in templates are left as-is in the rendered output (not removed, not throwing)

`BUILTIN_TEMPLATES` is frozen via `Object.freeze()` and contains prompt preambles for all 9 `AgentType` roles: architect, implementer, reviewer, tester, analyst, scrum_master, researcher, planner, documenter
Null-safety: handles undefined `roles`, missing `providerPrompts` map, and missing keys without throwing
`registerBuiltin()` allows overriding default templates for testing or plugin use
`registerBuiltin()` rejects forbidden keys (`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`) with thrown error (prototype pollution guard)
`registerBuiltin()` rejects overriding immutable roles (configured via `immutableRoles` in options)
`registerBuiltin()` logs WARN when overriding an existing template, INFO when adding a new one
`builtinTemplates` uses `Object.create(null)` as prototype-free backing store
`resolveTemplate()` validates `providerName` against forbidden keys and uses `Object.hasOwn()` for `providerPrompts` property access
Empty string `''` is treated as a valid template at any resolution level (not skipped)
Constructor accepts `AgentPromptRegistryOptions` object (with `config`, optional `logger`, optional `immutableRoles`) -- not bare `AgentsConfig`
`MAX_TEMPLATE_LENGTH` (1MB) and `MAX_VAR_VALUE_LENGTH` (100KB) size limits are enforced with warning logs
Works correctly with minimal config (`{ defaults: { providerChain: [] } }` with no roles, no systemPrompt, no providerPrompts)
Test file covers all 6 resolution levels, fallback to generic, variable interpolation, empty vars, registerBuiltin(), security guards, size limits, and edge cases
`pnpm --filter @tamma/providers run typecheck` passes
`pnpm vitest run packages/providers/src/agent-prompt-registry` passes

docs.doc: Story specification with full class design and code
AgentsConfig, AgentRoleConfig types (defines providerPrompts, systemPrompt fields)
Technical architecture, naming conventions, TypeScript strict mode

code.file: IAgentPromptRegistry interface, AgentPromptRegistryOptions, AgentPromptRegistry class with BUILTIN_TEMPLATES (frozen, 9 roles), GENERIC_FALLBACK, MAX_TEMPLATE_LENGTH, MAX_VAR_VALUE_LENGTH, FORBIDDEN_KEYS, resolveTemplate(), render(), registerBuiltin() with security guards
Unit tests for all 6 resolution levels, interpolation, null-safety, registerBuiltin(), security guards, size limits, prototype pollution, immutable roles, empty-string semantics
AgentType union type used as key for BUILTIN_TEMPLATES and resolveTemplate() role parameter
Existing PromptRegistry class (MCP prompt management) -- name collision to avoid
Existing prompt strings in generatePlan() and implementCode() -- source for BUILTIN_TEMPLATES extraction

dependencies.dependency: AgentType from knowledge.ts, AgentsConfig from agent-config.ts, ILogger from contracts

constraints.constraint: Class MUST be named `AgentPromptRegistry` implementing `IAgentPromptRegistry` to avoid collision with `PromptRegistry` in `packages/mcp-client/src/registry.ts`
All code must compile under TypeScript strict mode (strict: true, noImplicitAny, noImplicitReturns)
Files use kebab-case; classes use PascalCase; constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
Use `import type` for type-only imports; use `.js` extensions in import paths for ESM
Use split+join for interpolation, NOT regex, to prevent ReDoS on untrusted template content
Reject forbidden keys (`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`) in `registerBuiltin()` and `resolveTemplate()` to prevent prototype pollution
Use `Object.hasOwn()` for `providerPrompts` property access to guard against prototype pollution
Protect immutable security-critical role templates (e.g., `reviewer`) via `immutableRoles` option
`BUILTIN_TEMPLATES` is frozen via `Object.freeze()` -- constructor copies into `Object.create(null)` prototype-free instance field
Unreplaced `{{placeholders}}` are left as-is -- render() must not throw on missing vars
Export `IAgentPromptRegistry` interface matching pattern from IProviderHealthTracker (9-3), IAgentProviderFactory (9-4), IProviderChain (9-5)
Constructor accepts `AgentPromptRegistryOptions` object (not bare `AgentsConfig`) for extensibility with logger and immutableRoles

interface.name: AgentsConfig
interface.description: Multi-agent configuration: defaults (AgentRoleConfig, required), roles? (partial per-role overrides), phaseRoleMap?

interface.name: AgentRoleConfig
interface.description: Per-role config: providerChain (required), allowedTools?, maxBudgetUsd?, permissionMode?, systemPrompt?, providerPrompts? (Record<string, string> keyed by provider name)

standards.standard: 80% line coverage, 75% branch coverage, 85% function coverage
TDD: write tests before or alongside implementation
Mock external dependencies; no real API calls in unit tests

ideas.idea: Test level 1: per-provider-per-role prompt takes priority when roles[role].providerPrompts[providerName] is set
Test level 2: per-role systemPrompt is used when providerPrompts has no entry for the provider
Test level 3: defaults.providerPrompts[providerName] is used when role has no prompt config
Test level 4: defaults.systemPrompt is used when defaults.providerPrompts has no entry
Test level 5: builtinTemplates[role] is used when no config-level prompt exists
Test level 6: GENERIC_FALLBACK is returned when role has no built-in template and no config
Test render() replaces {{context}} and {{issueNumber}} with provided values
Test render() with multiple variables in one template
Test render() with empty vars object leaves {{placeholders}} unchanged
Test render() handles values containing regex special characters (e.g., $, ^, .)
Test render() handles values containing {{otherPlaceholder}} -- iterative expansion behavior is documented (B WILL be expanded within A's value if processed after A)
Test registerBuiltin() overrides a default template
Test registerBuiltin() adds a template for a role not in BUILTIN_TEMPLATES
Test registerBuiltin() rejects forbidden keys (__proto__, constructor, prototype) with thrown error
Test registerBuiltin() rejects overriding immutable roles with thrown error
Test registerBuiltin() logs WARN when overriding existing template, INFO when adding new
Test null-safety: config.roles is undefined
Test null-safety: config.roles[role] exists but has no providerPrompts
Test null-safety: config.defaults.providerPrompts is undefined
Test BUILTIN_TEMPLATES includes entries for all 9 AgentType roles: architect, implementer, reviewer, tester, analyst, scrum_master, researcher, planner, documenter
Test that each built-in template is a non-empty string
Test empty string '' is treated as a valid template override at every resolution level
Test resolveTemplate() rejects forbidden provider names (__proto__, constructor, prototype)
Test Object.hasOwn() guards on providerPrompts property access
Test interpolate() skips variables whose values exceed MAX_VAR_VALUE_LENGTH (100KB) with warning
Test rendered templates exceeding MAX_TEMPLATE_LENGTH (1MB) are truncated with warning
Test with minimal config: { defaults: { providerChain: [] } } with no roles, no systemPrompt, no providerPrompts -- falls through to built-in or generic
Test IAgentPromptRegistry interface is exported and AgentPromptRegistry implements it
Test constructor accepts AgentPromptRegistryOptions (not bare AgentsConfig)
